criteria.name: Required sections present
criteria.description: Check for standard PRD sections
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for: Summary, Problem Statement, Proposed Solution, User Stories, Acceptance Criteria, Out of Scope, NFRs

criteria.name: Scope boundaries defined
criteria.description: Check for explicit in-scope / out-of-scope sections
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for explicit in-scope / out-of-scope sections with specific boundary statements

criteria.name: Success criteria measurable
criteria.description: Check for quantitative language and verifiable outcomes
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for quantitative language, verifiable outcomes, or measurable thresholds in requirements

criteria.name: Story format compliance
criteria.description: Verify user story format
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Verify each user story follows 'As a ... I want ... So that ...' pattern (or close variant)

criteria.name: All stories have ACs
criteria.description: Check each story/epic has checkbox items
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check each story/epic has '- [ ]' checkbox items

criteria.name: Story numbering consistent
criteria.description: Verify sequential numbering and cross-references
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Verify epic/story numbering is sequential and cross-referenced correctly

criteria.name: Story priorities assigned
criteria.description: Check for priority indicators
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check if stories have priority indicators (P0/P1/P2) or priority ordering

criteria.name: Stories sized appropriately
criteria.description: Check AC count and scope complexity
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check AC count and scope complexity per story — flag stories with >8 ACs or overly broad scope

criteria.name: AC cover happy paths and error cases
criteria.description: Verify ACs include success and error handling
criteria.autoCheckMethod: For each story, verify ACs include both success scenarios and error/failure handling

criteria.name: Edge cases identified
criteria.description: Check for edge case mentions
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for edge case mentions in AC or body (boundary conditions, empty inputs, concurrent access, etc.)

criteria.name: Cross-references valid
criteria.description: Verify file paths and issue references exist
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Verify file paths, issue numbers, and proposal references exist

criteria.name: Performance requirements specified
criteria.description: Check NFR section for performance targets
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check NFR section for performance targets (response time, throughput, resource limits)

criteria.name: Security considerations documented
criteria.description: Check NFR section for security requirements
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check NFR section for security requirements (authentication, authorization, data handling, input validation)

criteria.name: Scalability or availability requirements
criteria.description: Check NFR section for scalability and availability
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check NFR section for scalability limits, availability targets, or capacity planning

criteria.name: Test plan presence
criteria.description: Check if test plan file exists alongside PRD
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check if test plan file exists alongside PRD (from Step 2)

criteria.name: AC coverage (if test plan)
criteria.description: Cross-reference ACs against test plan test cases
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Cross-reference each '- [ ]' AC against test plan test cases — report coverage %

criteria.name: Integration test scenarios (if test plan)
criteria.description: Check test plan for integration test cases
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check test plan for integration test cases covering component interactions

criteria.name: E2E test scenarios (if test plan)
criteria.description: Check test plan for end-to-end scenarios
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check test plan for end-to-end scenarios covering critical user journeys from the PRD

criteria.name: Test coverage approach documented
criteria.description: Check test plan for coverage targets
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check test plan for coverage targets and testing strategy section

criteria.name: Test coverage proportionate
criteria.description: Verify test ACs proportionate to story scope
criteria.autoCheckMethod: For each user story, verify that ACs include test-related requirements proportionate to the story's scope. Stories that add or modify code paths should have ACs requiring unit tests, integration tests, or E2E tests as appropriate. Flag stories with complex scope (multiple files, refactoring, new modules) whose ACs lack any test requirement.

criteria.name: Screen spec alignment (UI PRDs)
criteria.description: Verify PRD UI requirements align with referenced screen specs
criteria.autoCheckMethod: If the PRD references screen specs (via `## Screen Specs` in the source proposal or direct `Screen-Specs/` references), verify that PRD acceptance criteria for UI features align with the referenced screen specs. Flag any PRD requirement that introduces UI elements not present in the proposal's screen specs. If no screen spec references exist, skip this check (non-blocking).

subjective.name: Acceptance criteria quality
subjective.question: Are the acceptance criteria specific enough to be independently testable? (Auto-checks verified structure; this asks about semantic quality.)

options.label: Testable ✅
options.description: ACs are specific, measurable, and independently verifiable

options.label: Mostly testable ⚠️
options.description: Most ACs are testable but some are vague or overlapping

options.label: Not testable ❌
options.description: ACs are too vague, subjective, or not independently verifiable

subjective.name: Problem decomposition
subjective.question: Does the PRD adequately decompose the problem — are the epics/stories the right granularity for this scope?

options.label: Well decomposed ✅
options.description: Stories are right-sized, epics group logically, nothing missing

options.label: Minor issues ⚠️
options.description: Some stories too large or grouping could improve

options.label: Needs rework ❌
options.description: Stories too coarse, missing key functionality, or poorly grouped

_meta.note: PRD review criteria for /review-prd. Extracted from inline command spec table.
